Note: u6/244/a2
was not clocked
in

Older
& 2019

Anita

CASITAS
Municipal Water District

1055 Ventura Avenue
P.O. Box 37
Oak View, CA 93022
(805) 649-2251
Fax (805) 649-3001

June 16, 1992

Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

RE: Potential Conflicts of Interest

Gentlemen:

Questions have arisen in connection with the possibility that two individuals
who service the Casitas Municipal Water District, hereinafter "Casitas", under
independent contractor's contracts and who also serve on the City Council of
the City of Ojai, a city wholly within the borders of Casitas, may have conflicts
of interest. The purpose of this letter is to request an opinion on these matters
from your legal staff.

The facts are as follows:

Case 1
Robert N. McKinney is a registered civil engineer who retired from
Casitas in January, 1987 after serving Casitas for approximately 23
years, the last 20 years as the General Manager and Chief Engineer.
During that time as a part of his duties as General Manager and Chief
Engineer, he attended meetings in Sacramento and elsewhere of the
State Water Contractors on behalf of Casitas, United Water Conservation
District, hereinafter "United", and the City of Ventura, hereinafter
"Ventura", each of which has through agreements allocations of State
water. Upon Mr. McKinney's retirement with the approval of United
and Ventura, both of whom agreed to pay a portion of the expenses,
Casitas contracted with Mr. McKinney to continue to perform the
services which he had been performing on an intermittent part-time basis
representing the three agencies at meetings of the State Water
Contractors. Mr. McKinney's services are advisory only and limited to
the State Water Project. In 1987, none of these three agencies was
taking a supply of State water, although each had a contractual
allocation. Approximately a year and a half ago, United began taking a
part of the State water to which it has a contractual allocation. Casitas
and Ventura still do not take any part of the State water. The State
Water Contractors oversee the management by the State Department of
Water Resources of the State Water Project and has been responsible for

Bill Hicks
Division I

William L. Austin
Division II

Laurence R. Whelan
Division III

Al Miles
Division IV

James W. Coultas
Division V

John J. Johnson
General Manager

James D. Loebl
Attorney

Ronald E. Morse
Auditor

FPPC Letter
June 16, 1992
Page 2

negotiating with that department to reduce the cost of operation and
resolving disputes. Approximately a year and a half ago, United once those
not to renew its contract with Casitas to pay a portion of Mr.
McKinney's services, though United still receives some reports on the
Contractors' meetings. Since his retirement, Mr. McKinney has
contracted as indicated above with Casitas on an annual basis. The
most recent contract is attached hereto. In its composition, scope of
duties, etc. it is typical of his previous contracts.

Mr. McKinney has also become a member of the Ojai City Council. Mr.
McKinney has also become mayor of the City of Ojai. Mr. McKinney has
indicated that he does not report to the City Council either directly or
indirectly on any of the matters relating to his contract with Casitas
concerning the Contractors. The City of Ojai has a franchise with the
Southerler California Water Company to supply municipal and industrial
water to the City and its residents. A very small portion of the City may
be supplied directly by Casitas. Casitas is a back-up supply for the
Southern California Water Company when supplies are required.

Based upon 1991 legislation SB534, the Ojai Groundwater Management
Agency was formed to manage the Ojai Groundwater Basin. This basin
lies wholly within Casitas boundaries and partially within the City of Ojai.
The Board of Directors includes representatives of the Southern California
Water Company, Casitas, and the City of Ojai. A Casitas Board member
represents Casitas while Mr. McKinney represents the City of Ojai. The
issues involved in this groundwater basin may involve differences of
opinion between the City of Ojai and Casitas.

Question: Do Mr. McKinney's services as a member of the City Council
of Ojai, as a representative of the City of Ojai on the Ojai Groundwater
Management Agency, and as a contractor with Casitas constitute a
conflict of interest? Are there any issues which foreseeably raise a
conflict of interest between his duties to Casitas and his service on the
Council of the City of Ojai or on the Board of the Ojai Groundwater
Management Agency?

Question: Are there any foreseeable times when Mr. McKinney should
state a conflict of interest and refuse to participate either on the City
Council or as an independent contractor with Casitas or as a member of
the Ojai Groundwater Management Agency? Is refusing to participate
enough?

FPPC Letter
June 16, 1992
Page 3

Case 2
The second case involves the services of James D. Loebl, who has
served as Attorney for Casitas since July, 1964. Mr. Loebl has also
served as a member of the Ojai City Council since April, 1968. A copy
of Mr. Loebl's most recent contract with Casitas is attached hereto. Mr.
Loebl was appointed by the Board of Directors of Casitas pursuant to
Section 71340 of the Water Code. His duties consist of advising the
Board and the General Manager when requested to do so by the General
Manager on all legal matters. He drafts or reviews contracts,
agreements, resolutions and other such documents as may be given to
him for review or preparation by the General Manager or the Board. He
also prepares and gives opinions, either orally or in writing, as requested
by the Board and the General Manager.

From time to time, matters involving water law and public employment
law have been dealt with by Mr. Loebl or in some instances have been
referred to outside counsel for disposition. In addition, public liability
cases are usually, if not exclusively, referred to counsel assigned by
Casitas' insurance carrier.

Mr. Loebl, in matters which involve Casitas' relations with the City of
Ojai, routinely declares a conflict of interest and refuses to participate.
We understand that while serving on the City Council, Mr. Loebl also
declares a conflict of interest and refuses to participate in matters
involving Casitas.

However, since the development of the Ojai Groundwater Management
Agency in 1991, the Ojai City Council has, by its participation, become
more involved in water issues. It is presumed that differences of opinion
concerning groundwater management could occur between Casitas and
the City of Ojai.

Question: Do Mr. Loebl's services as a member of the City Council of
Ojai and as a contract attorney for Casitas constitute a conflict of
interest? Are there any issues which foreseeably raise a conflict of
interest between his duties to Casitas and his service on the Ojai City
Council?

Question: Are there any foreseeable times when Mr. Loebl should state
a conflict of interest and refuse to participate either on the City Council

FPPC Letter
June 16, 1992
Page 4

or as an independent contractor with Casitas? Is refusing to participate
enough?

In summary, we would like the FPPC's opinion as to whether there is a
conflict of interest constituted by the concerns and questions described above.
We look forward to hearing from you and receiving your response to this
request at your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
John J. Johnson
General Manager


JJJ:ep
Attach. (2)

sections.question: Do Mr. McKinney's services as a member of the City Council of Ojai, as a representative of the City of Ojai on the Ojai Groundwater Management Agency, and as a contractor with Casitas constitute a conflict of interest? Are there any issues which foreseeably raise a conflict of interest between his duties to Casitas and his service on the Council of the City of Ojai or on the Board of the Ojai Groundwater Management Agency? Are there any foreseeable times when Mr. McKinney should state a conflict of interest and refuse to participate either on the City Council or as an independent contractor with Casitas or as a member of the Ojai Groundwater Management Agency? Is refusing to participate enough? Do Mr. Loebl's services as a member of the City Council of Ojai and as a contract attorney for Casitas constitute a conflict of interest? Are there any issues which foreseeably raise a conflict of interest between his duties to Casitas and his service on the Ojai City Council? Are there any foreseeable times when Mr. Loebl should state a conflict of interest and refuse to participate either on the City Council or as an independent contractor with Casitas? Is refusing to participate enough?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether two individuals (Robert McKinney and James Loebl) who serve as independent contractors to the Casitas Municipal Water District while simultaneously serving on the City Council of Ojai have conflicts of interest, particularly regarding the newly formed Ojai Groundwater Management Agency which may create disputes between Casitas and the City of Ojai.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter, not an FPPC response. The document contains two separate cases with questions embedded within narrative text rather than formal Q&A sections. No conclusion or FPPC response is present. The document quality is somewhat degraded (appears to be a scan) but content is legible.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Do Mr. McKinney's services as a member of the City Council of Ojai, as a representative of the City of Ojai on the Ojai Groundwater Management Agency, and as a contractor with Casitas constitute a conflict of interest? Are there any issues which foreseeably raise a conflict of interest between his duties to Casitas and his service on the Council of the City of Ojai or on the Board of the Ojai Groundwater Management Agency? Are there any foreseeable times when Mr. McKinney should state a conflict of interest and refuse to participate either on the City Council or as an independent contractor with Casitas or as a member of the Ojai Groundwater Management Agency? Is refusing to participate enough? Do Mr. Loebl's services as a member of the City Council of Ojai and as a contract attorney for Casitas constitute a conflict of interest? Are there any issues which foreseeably raise a conflict of interest between his duties to Casitas and his service on the Ojai City Council? Are there any foreseeable times when Mr. Loebl should state a conflict of interest and refuse to participate either on the City Council or as an independent contractor with Casitas? Is refusing to participate enough?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Casitas Municipal Water District to the FPPC dated June 16, 1992, seeking an opinion on potential conflicts of interest involving two individuals who simultaneously serve as contractors to Casitas and as City Council members of Ojai.
